home.positioning: AI-native product engineer — I ship LLM features 0-to-1.
home.bio: Six-plus years shipping full products solo — frontend, backend, infra, the lot. Now I build AI-native products end to end: from prompt design and eval harnesses to the production app around them.

settle.title: AI bill review with a human in the loop
settle.outcome: Claude vision extracts and reconciles every line item before a human signs off.

hiring-radar.title: Hybrid search that doubles recall
hiring-radar.outcome: Lexical + vector ranking surfaces strong candidates that pure semantic search misses.

daily-news.title: RAG inside a production mobile app
daily-news.outcome: Local embeddings + Groq deliver fast, cited answers from within the app.

how-i-build-with-ai.title: My Claude Code workflow
how-i-build-with-ai.outcome: How I orchestrate agents and MCP tooling to ship production code at IOHK.

about.title: Senior full-stack product engineer, now building AI-native products end to end.
about.intro: For six-plus years I've shipped full products solo — data model, APIs, frontend, infra, tests. The AI direction builds on that; it doesn't replace it. I'm not pivoting away from engineering into prompting — I'm adding the AI layer to work that already had to run in production. The same instincts that kept a fintech app stable at scale are the ones that keep an LLM feature honest.
about.how: I take features from problem to production — data model and APIs, the frontend, the infra, the tests, and now the AI layer: prompts, evals, retrieval, and the product around them. I work best as the technical owner of a surface, deciding where deterministic code ends and a model begins. And I document as I go, so the work and the reasoning behind it outlive me.

iohk.name: Input Output (IOHK)
iohk.body: On Lace, the multi-chain wallet, I architected a contract-module system that lets new chains and dApp integrations slot in without touching the core. Separately, I built the team's Claude Code framework — the agents and tooling other engineers now use day to day.

bolster.name: Bolster
bolster.body: Joined as the fourth engineer and built greenfield fintech surfaces from zero. I owned the core mobile experience as it grew to 100K+ users in seven months, then went back and made it fast and stable — cutting cold-start time and crashes hard.

globant.name: Globant
globant.body: Full-stack engineer on Lessen, a US proptech platform. I built the shared React Native component library that the product teams built on — standardizing UI and cutting cross-platform effort by roughly a third.

moony.name: Moony
moony.body: Co-founded and built the product end to end — every layer, every decision. The crash course in owning a thing from idea to shipped that everything since has built on.

work.notTranslated: Showing the original version of this case study.

footer.positioning: AI-native product engineer — I ship LLM features 0-to-1.
